spec_match.rationale: The demo clearly shows the behavior of all text-fit values (none, contain, scale-down) and bounded sizes side-by-side.

interactive.rationale: The container width range slider provides a simple and immediate way to observe the responsive scaling differences.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The index blurb perfectly maps to the demo's purpose of comparing the various text-fit values side-by-side.

edge_cases.rationale: Because only one static text ('Headline Text') is used, the critical difference that 'scale-down' does not grow short text while 'contain' does is not fully demonstrated.

references.rationale: The reference list successfully includes the target ChromeStatus entry link.

design_system.rationale: The demo complies with the layout, CSS variable structure, and typography of the global design system.

openQuestions.title: Add custom text inputs or predefined text length presets
openQuestions.detail: To clearly demonstrate that 'scale-down' only shrinks when text overflows its container, but does not grow short text (unlike 'contain'), we should allow the user to switch between short text ('Short'), standard text ('Headline Text'), and very long text ('An Extremely Bounded Long Headline Text That Will Overload the Container').

summary: A clean, elegant side-by-side comparison page of the standard text-fit values that utilizes a container width slider to demonstrate the core spec rules. It can be polished by introducing user-defined text or text length presets to better illustrate the difference between scale-down and contain.
